Reading the grid: fill colour = where the unit sits in the lifecycle (Occupied → Notice → Evict → Vacant → Turning → Marketing → Deposit Hold → Occupied); outline = how far behind on rent; glyph = months in arrears with the payment direction since the prior report (a tenant one month behind who keeps paying shows 1.0▼, not a red flag). Occupied cells show scheduled rent, vacant cells show market/advertised rent. Amber dots = work orders in that report period (KPM: exact unit-attributed maintenance $74,037 across 2025; Edge: new WOs that week, first Edge column is a trailing window). WO$ '25 column = unit-attributed KPM maintenance spend. Narrow 2023–24 columns come from a status-only weekly archive (no money fields — nothing invented). Sources: PDG weekly rolls + archive, KPM owner-statement packets, Edge weekly XLSX (rent roll, delinquency w/ aging, vacancy detail w/ rent-ready, work orders).
